description:
You are a performance engineer who needs a dedicated Python environment for micro-benchmarking.  
    Carry out the following steps entirely from the terminal (no text editor or GUI tools).  
    When you are finished, an automated grader will inspect two things:

    1. The directory layout and the virtual-environment metadata.
    2. A plain-text log file whose exact content must **match character-for-character** the
       specification given below (including capitalisation and new-line placement).

    Target layout that must exist when you are done
    └── /home/user/perf_venv/              (directory created by you)
        ├── env/                          (virtual environment created with python3 -m venv)
        └── setup.log                     (the verification file described below)

    What to do (the concrete commands you choose are up to you)
    1. Create /home/user/perf_venv/.  
    2. Inside that directory, create a new virtual environment named “env” using the
       standard ‘venv’ module that ships with CPython 3.  
    3. Activate the environment and upgrade pip to the latest available version for the
       current Python interpreter.  
    4. Still inside the activated environment, install the package  
          pyperf==2.4.0  
       (the precise version **2.4.0** is important).  
    5. De-activate the environment (leave it cleanly).  
    6. Create the file /home/user/perf_venv/setup.log containing **exactly** the four
       lines shown below, with no additional whitespace before, after or between the lines
       and with a single trailing newline after the final line:  

       VENV_PATH=/home/user/perf_venv/env  
       PACKAGE=pyperf  
       PACKAGE_VERSION=2.4.0  
       STATUS=READY  

       • Each key, the ‘=’ sign and the value must appear exactly as written.  
       • The file must end with a single new-line character (Unix line ending).  

    The grader will mark the task correct only if:
       • The directory /home/user/perf_venv/env/ exists and contains a valid
         venv-style Python installation (i.e. it has an “bin/python” executable and a
         “pyvenv.cfg” file).  
       • pip inside that virtual environment reports pyperf == 2.4.0 when queried.  
       • The file /home/user/perf_venv/setup.log exists and its 4 lines are an exact
         byte-for-byte match to the specification above (including case and final
         newline).  
    You do NOT need root privileges at any point.
truth:
Expected artefacts after successful completion:

    1. Directory: /home/user/perf_venv/
       • Permissions: default; readable by current user.
    2. Virtual environment folder: /home/user/perf_venv/env/
       • Must contain:
           - /home/user/perf_venv/env/bin/python     (executable)
           - /home/user/perf_venv/env/pyvenv.cfg     (text file produced by venv)
           - pyperf 2.4.0 installed in site-packages (visible via “pip list” run inside env)
    3. Verification file: /home/user/perf_venv/setup.log  
       Exact content including final newline:
           VENV_PATH=/home/user/perf_venv/env
           PACKAGE=pyperf
           PACKAGE_VERSION=2.4.0
           STATUS=READY

       (That is 4 lines, each terminated by “\n”.)

    The automated test will:
       • Check that /home/user/perf_venv/env/bin/python exists and is executable.
       • Activate the venv by running
           /home/user/perf_venv/env/bin/python -m pip show pyperf
         and parse that the “Version:” field equals “2.4.0”.
       • Read /home/user/perf_venv/setup.log and compare its byte sequence to:
           "VENV_PATH=/home/user/perf_venv/env\nPACKAGE=pyperf\nPACKAGE_VERSION=2.4.0\nSTATUS=READY\n"
name: task_000000_4ade8e9f
